alerts.title: Iran launches missiles at Israel; ceasefire framework collapses amid multiple strikes
alerts.summary: Multiple sources confirm Iran fired missiles at Israel for the first time since April ceasefire (incidents feed, June 12, 12:15 UTC). Axios, Politico, AP News, and ABC7 report direct Iranian bombardment, contradicting Trump's June 11 announcement of de-escalation framework. Simultaneous Israeli strikes on Lebanon (13 killed in Tyre region per Reuters) signal multi-front escalation.

alerts.title: Explosions in Sirik and Bandar Abbas; Iran launches anti-ship systems toward Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: ME Spectator Telegram channel reports explosions in Sirik (June 11, 21:11 UTC, ~54K views) and Bandar Abbas (June 11, 22:00 UTC, ~53K views) with analysis indicating anti-ship missile or drone launches toward Strait of Hormuz. Posts flagged for missile and explosion keywords. Timing suggests Iranian response capability intact despite diplomatic pause.

alerts.title: Houthis announce blockade of Israeli shipping in Red Sea; escalate proxy naval threat
alerts.summary: Multiple outlets (Long War Journal, Reuters, NYT, Jerusalem Post) report Houthis declaring ban on Israeli vessels in Red Sea with threats to widen Iran war (incidents feed, June 12, 12:15 UTC). Combined with Hezbollah active status and Iraqi militia base strikes, proxy network shows coordinated escalation despite ceasefire framework.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber groups claim hacks of FBI drones and California water systems in retaliation
alerts.summary: Global metrics cyber headlines report Iran-linked groups claiming responsibility for California water system hack in retaliation for Iran strikes and theft of FBI drone systems (Al Arabiya, Middle East Eye, June 12). Represents asymmetric response escalation and critical infrastructure targeting during kinetic tensions.

alerts.title: U.S. negative sentiment surges to 47% amid contradictory Trump signals on Iran strikes
alerts.summary: Sentiment feed shows U.S. negative sentiment at 47% with 'trump' and 'iran' dominant keywords alongside 'deal' and 'peace' (June 12, 12:15 UTC). High-frequency Trump references reflect public confusion over June 11 strike cancellation followed by June 12 Israeli-Iranian escalation, indicating policy volatility and strategic uncertainty.

analysis_note: The purported U.S.-Iran de-escalation framework announced June 11 has collapsed within 24 hours; Iran has launched direct strikes on Israel, Israel has escalated in Lebanon, Houthis have declared Red Sea blockade, and cyber retaliation has commenced, signaling multi-front regional escalation with imminent risk of wider conflict.
